summary: The diff implements the vast majority of requirements for the PS Sadar Bazar Application Box. All 10 requirements show substantial implementation evidence. A few acceptance criteria have gaps: the backend OTP simulation is stored in localStorage rather than backend state (a constraint violation for REQ-2); the hearing date/time is not clearly stored in the backend status log entry (StatusLogEntry type lacks hearingDate/hearingTime fields in the visible backend code); and some minor concerns about backend attendance storage.

missing_requirements.requirement: Backend stores and validates the mobile OTP (simulated) before allowing password update.
missing_requirements.reason: The OTP simulation is implemented entirely in localStorage on the frontend (useAuth.ts, OTP_KEY), not in backend state (main.mo). The constraint explicitly states OTP records must be stored in the backend state.

missing_requirements.requirement: Admin can set a hearing date and time when updating status; all status changes are logged with hearing date/time in the backend status log.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend StatusLogEntry type in main.mo only contains status, updatedAt, updatedBy, and details fields with no hearingDate or hearingTime. The frontend form collects these values but there is no evidence they are passed to or stored by the backend updateComplaintStatus call.

hallucinated_features.description: Designation field added to Officer management
hallucinated_features.reason: REQ-6 only specifies officer name and mobile number for officer appointment. The implementation adds a designation field to the Officer type and the add officer form, which was not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Custom camera hook with full video preview dialog instead of input[capture=camera]
hallucinated_features.reason: REQ-4 AC specifies camera capture via input[capture=camera] for mobile devices. The implementation uses a custom useCamera hook with a video stream dialog and canvas capture, going beyond the specified simpler approach.
